description:
You are a newly-hired database reliability engineer.  
    Your first assignment is to perform a quick “security hygiene” scan on the existing backup
    directory and file a report for the senior DBA.

    Background
    ----------
    • All database dump files are stored under  
      /home/user/backups/  
      (several test files already exist there).  
    • A file is considered **insecure** if **either the group or the “other” permission
      set has the write bit enabled** (that is, if the octal file mode has a 2 or 6 in
      the group or other position).

    What you must do
    ----------------
    1. Create the directory /home/user/backup_audit/ if it does not yet exist.  
    2. Scan every regular file directly contained in /home/user/backups/ (ignore
       sub-directories or dot-files).  
    3. Detect every file that is insecure as defined above.  
    4. Produce a log file named  
       /home/user/backup_audit/backup_security_report.log  
       following *exactly* the formatting rules shown below.  
    5. Leave all existing files untouched; do **not** change permissions, owners or
       timestamps.  
    6. The entire task can be completed with roughly 5-10 normal shell commands
       (find, stat, awk, etc.)—no need for a full script, but you may create one if you
       prefer.

    Required log-file format
    ------------------------
    • Plain ASCII text.  
    • First block: one line per insecure file, sorted in lexicographical order
      by filename (not by full path).  
    • Each line in that block must be:
          <relative-filename>,<octal-mode>,<size-in-bytes>
      Do not insert spaces anywhere in that line. The relative filename is the file
      name exactly as it appears in /home/user/backups/ (no leading “./”).  
    • After the last file line, insert a single completely blank line.  
    • Then append exactly two summary lines:  
          Total insecure files: <count>  
          Scan completed successfully.  
      (There must be one space after the colon.)

    Example of **precise** expected structure (values will differ):
        bad_dump.sql,666,12074
        old_test.dump,606,48123

        Total insecure files: 2
        Scan completed successfully.

    Verification notes
    ------------------
    • The automated grader will look only at  
      /home/user/backup_audit/backup_security_report.log  
      and will compare it byte-for-byte against the expected content, including the
      trailing newline after “Scan completed successfully.”  
    • The grader will also verify that the permissions of the original files remain
      unchanged.  
    • You do not need root privileges; everything can be done as the normal user.  
    • Feel free to emit normal console output while working—the grader ignores
      terminal chatter.

    Deliverable
    -----------
    The single file /home/user/backup_audit/backup_security_report.log constructed
    exactly as specified above.
truth:
Initial filesystem state created by the test harness
    ----------------------------------------------------
    Directories (all owned by user:user, mode 755 unless noted):
      /home/user/backups/
      /home/user/backup_scripts/               (not used in the task)

    Files inside /home/user/backups/ (sizes are real byte counts, modes are octal):
      prod_2023-09-01.sql     —  size: 12345  mode: 600
      prod_2023-09-02.sql     —  size: 23456  mode: 660   (insecure)
      prod_2023-09-03.sql     —  size: 34567  mode: 666   (insecure)
      staging_2023-09-01.dump —  size: 45678  mode: 644
      staging_2023-09-02.dump —  size: 56789  mode: 606   (insecure)

    Expected deliverable created by the agent
    -----------------------------------------
    File: /home/user/backup_audit/backup_security_report.log
    Mode: 644  (agent may rely on default umask to achieve this)

    Exact byte-for-byte content of that file (including a final newline at EOF):

        prod_2023-09-02.sql,660,23456
        prod_2023-09-03.sql,666,34567
        staging_2023-09-02.dump,606,56789

        Total insecure files: 3
        Scan completed successfully.

    (The file ends with a single “\n” after the word “successfully.”)

    Other verifications the grader will perform
    -------------------------------------------
    • /home/user/backup_audit/ must exist and be a directory.  
    • No permissions of the five original backup files may differ from the table
      above.  
    • The agent does not need to create or modify any file other than the required
      log; creation of temporary files in /tmp is tolerated but ignored during 
      grading.
